title: thelypteris malabariensis
intro: Thelypteris malabariensis, also known as the Malabar maiden fern, is a species of fern found in the Western Ghats of India. This plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Thelypteris malabariensis include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by drying and powdering the fronds, which can be used to make teas, decoctions, or capsules.
